steps.title: Interpret the equation as a rate × hours problem
steps.content: The equation $22h + 18c = 1,164$ has the form (rate) × (hours) + (rate) × (hours) = total. The variable $h$ represents hours at the regular job, $c$ represents hours at the second job, and the total equals $1,164$ dollars.

steps.title: Identify what multiplies the regular job hours
steps.content: In an earnings equation, the coefficient multiplying hours must be the hourly rate. Since 22 is the coefficient of $h$ (regular job hours), the value 22 represents the hourly rate at the regular job, expressed in dollars per hour.

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content: The correct answer states the amount, in dollars, the mechanic earned for each hour she worked at her regular job. This describes the hourly wage at the regular job, which is the value 22. That is the correct answer.
